channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Nathan Ordaz scores, LAFC blanks Leo Messi and Inter Miami 1-0 in 1st leg of CONCACAF Champions Cup
item.description: Los Angeles FC kept Lionel Messi off the scoresheet in a 1-0 victory over Inter Miami in the first leg of their CONCACAF Champions Cup quarterfinal matchup.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Houston blows past the Jazz, 143-105
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - The end of the season can't come soon enough for the Utah Jazz. Jalen Green scored 22 points, while Alperen Sengun was one assist away from a triple-double, as the Houston Rockets crushed the Jazz Wednesday night, 143-105.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ohtani's walk-off homer in 9th completes rally as Dodgers stun winless Braves 6-5 for 8-0 start
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani hit a tiebreaking home run in the ninth inning on his bobblehead night, lifting the unbeaten Los Angeles Dodgers over the winless Atlanta Braves 6-5 on Wednesday. The Dodgers improved to 8-0, the best start ever by a defending World Series champion.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: San Jose Giants beat Oakland Ballers in Bay Area baseball exhibition, evoking memories of original Battle of the Bay
item.description: Oakland Ballers, San Jose Giants look to fill void of lost Bay Area baseball rivalry with "Battle of the Bay 2.0" in San Jose
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Ovechkin scores his 892nd career goal. He needs 2 to tie Gretzky and 3 to break the NHL record
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Alex Ovechkin took another step closer to breaking Wayne Gretzky's NHL record by scoring his 892nd career goal in the Washington Capitals' 5-1 loss at the Carolina Hurricanes on Wednesday night. Ovechkin moved two away from tying Gretzky and three from passing him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: All 5 starters out for exhausted Nuggets against Spurs following double-0T heartbreaker
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Denver coach Michael Malone declared all five of his starters out for the exhausted Nuggets' game against the San Antonio Spurs on Wednesday night. Denver's 140-139 double-overtime home loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves on Tuesday night played a role in Malone resting his stars.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Phillies' Matt Strahm calls for pitchers to use pine tar again in response to torpedo bat craze
item.description: Matt Strahm said in a recent X post that as long as torpedo bats are around, pitchers should be able to use "whatever hitters have in the on-deck circle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Curry hits 12 3s and scores 52 as Warriors moving up in the West with a victory over the Grizzlies
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — The last time Stephen Curry and Golden State played in Memphis, he was held without a field goal and the Warriors lost by 51 points. Curry didn’t recall the frustration from that loss in December. And there was no repeat on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Paul Skenes dominates the Rays on the day GQ publishes profile of him and girlfriend Livvy Dunne
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — The only thing that flustered Paul Skenes on Wednesday was getting asked about his photo spread with girlfriend Livvy Dunne in GQ magazine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Super Bowl champion John Vella dead at 74
item.description: John Vella, a former Oakland Raiders and Minnesota Vikings standout offensive lineman, has died, the Las Vegas team announced. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: High school track athlete, 16, fatally stabbed at championship meet, officials say
item.description: A 16-year-old high school track athlete was allegedly killed by a member of another team during a championship meet Wednesday in Frisco, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British tennis pro born with rare genetic disorder collapses mid-match in terrifying scene
item.description: Francesca Jones, a British tennis pro born with a rare genetic disorder, collapsed during a match in Colombia on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yanic Konan Neiderhauser declares for NBA draft
item.description: UNIVERSITY PARK (Nittany Nation) -- One of the key returning pieces for Penn State basketball next season may not be returning after all, now that 7-footer Yanic Konan Neiderhauser declared for the NBA draft Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Clay, PHL 17

item.title: Washington athletics org's proposals to change trans-athlete policy would violate state law, official says
item.description: Washington's high school athletics organization is unable to enact proposals that would keep biological males from girls sports because they would violate state law, an official said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jokic scores career-high 61 points, plays nearly 53 minutes in 140-139 double-OT loss to Wolves
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Michael Malone called him Superman. At the moment, Nikola Jokic’s just super-tired. The Denver Nuggets big man can't wait to get some sleep after a career-best 61-point performance in which he played nearly 53 minutes.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers improve to 7-0 behind Dustin May after beating winless Braves 3-1
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Dustin May pitched five strong innings after missing nearly two years, Mookie Betts hit his third go-ahead homer of the season and the Los Angeles Dodgers beat the winless Atlanta Braves 3-1 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Curry hits 12 3-pointers, scores 52 as Warriors defeat Grizzlies 134-125
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Stephen Curry had 52 points, including 12 3-pointers, as the Golden State Warriors held on for a 134-125 victory over the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Padres pick up another win, making for single best start to season ever
item.description: The San Diego Padres have made history after beating the Cleveland Guardians 7-2 at Petco Park on Monday, giving them a five-game winning streak to start the season for the first time ever.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Kanon Catchings enters transfer portal
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After a disappointing freshman season, BYU forward Kanon Catchings has entered the transfer portal. The highly-touted 4-star recruit came to BYU with high expectations. But injuries and lack of playing time derailed his freshman season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Hailey Van Lith scores 26 points to lead TCU to 1st Elite Eight with 71-62 win over Notre Dame
item.description: TCU is in unchartered territory thanks to Hailey Van Lith, reaching the Elite Eight for the first time.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bulls face the Mavericks on 4-game win streak
item.description: Dallas Mavericks (36-38, 10th in the Western Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (33-40, ninth in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: Chicago aims to keep its four-game win streak intact when the Bulls take on Dallas. The Bulls are 14-22 in home games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Green Bay Packer charged for alleged dogfighting
item.description: The standout college football running back played 14 games for the Green and Gold between 1994 and 1995.
item.dc:creator: Nick Witwer, NewsNation

item.title: SF's Chase Center hosting NCAA Men's Basketball games Thursday, Saturday
item.description: (BCN) -- San Francisco is hosting two games at Chase Center on Thursday in the NCAA Men's Basketball Championship tournament. At 4:39 p.m., University of Florida, which is the No. 1 seed in the West region of the tournament's bracket, plays No. 4 University of Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: NCAA Tournament regionals: A look at Chicagoland connections, program history and more for the men's basketball Sweet 16
item.description: The 2025 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament resumes Thursday night, and it's down to the Sweet 16. It'll be the Elite Eight after the regional semifinals on Thursday and Friday and then the Final Four after the regional finals on Saturday and Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
